mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show negative mean or Rank IC below 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows when FEDFUNDS declines.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending economic contraction, triggering risk-off flows and negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in > 55% of instances following the trigger threshold, or the signal loses predictive power during expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, driving corporate revenue growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns when GDPC1 growth exceeds 2% but RSAFS declines, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates resilient manufacturing output that offsets monetary tightening pressures, generating short-term positive S&P 500 forward returns as markets price in sustained earnings.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns turn negative during tightening cycles despite rising INDPRO, or the signal reverses direction when inflation exceeds 4%.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated consumer price indices combined with suppressed federal funds rates compress real yields and erode purchasing power, forcing equity repricing and negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns dominate when CPIAUCSL exceeds 230 and FEDFUNDS remains below 1%, or the interaction effect vanishes in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic capacity and wage growth, supporting corporate profit margins and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over three months.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show no statistical correlation with PAYEMS growth, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month decline in housing starts signals weakening residential investment and broader credit tightening, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns as growth expectations are downgraded.
failure_condition: The threshold trigger fails to predict negative 20-day returns in > 60% of occurrences, or housing market decouples from equity performance during rate hike cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in industrial production and decreases in unemployment indicate efficient labor reallocation and supply chain normalization, driving short-term positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 5-day returns when INDPRO rises but UNRATE simultaneously increases, or the interaction term lacks predictive significance in out-of-sample tests.
